danger_signals.visual_form: Biologically engineered creature sprites; visually distinct from natural fauna
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threat; system malfunction; unnatural contamination of Mota

danger_signals.visual_form: Arima displayed as destroyed urban environment with collapsed structures
danger_signals.meaning: Violence, lawlessness, and societal breakdown; indicates hoodlum activity and loss of central authority control

danger_signals.visual_form: Mechanical enemy sprites in later game areas
danger_signals.meaning: Authoritarian control; oppression by Mother Brain system; false accusations of sabotage

danger_signals.visual_form: Isolated space structure environment distinct from planetary zones
danger_signals.meaning: Captivity, state punishment, loss of freedom; collision/explosion indicates instability of Mother Brain's control systems

safety_signals.visual_form: Lush, green terraformed landscape; artificial structures providing climate control
safety_signals.meaning: Technological harmony; artificial peace; dependence on central system; comfortable but complacent civilization

safety_signals.visual_form: Organized government city with military/administrative presence
safety_signals.meaning: Seat of authority; safe zone of established order; point of mission briefing and societal structure

safety_signals.visual_form: Character sprites joining party progressively throughout overworld exploration
safety_signals.meaning: Building alliance; gathering diverse skills and perspectives; safety through numbers and specialization

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Security robots, terraformed landscapes, domed agricultural structures
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: BioSystem lab, Climacontrol facility, Gaila prison satellite, Noah spaceship; highly technological and organized spaces

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Spaceship Noah; command authority from destroyed Earth
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Captain and crew members as final antagonists; space pirate Tyler (initially allied, later revealed as outside faction)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Noah spaceship; implies external colonial/imperial presence; destroyed Earth origin

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mota planet; government agent insignia (Rolf's identity)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hoodlums in Shure and Arima (corrupted/desperate citizens)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Paseo capital, towns, agricultural zones, BioSystem lab; represents peaceful but complacent society

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Esper Mansion on Dezo; connection to original hero Alis
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Neifirst as twisted predecessor of Nei; represents resistance to Mother Brain
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Esper Mansion on planet Dezo; isolated magical/spiritual location; refuge outside Mother Brain's direct control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lush green terraformed Mota; organized towns; domed farms; functioning infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs live complacent lives; civilization has forgotten ancient heroes and conflicts; low vigilance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Biomonster sprites appear in overworld and dungeons; environmental hazards; visual corruption of natural areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy encounters; towns become less safe; panic among citizens; mission urgency established

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Destroyed architecture; ruined town layout; hoodlum presence; visible decay and violence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Darum turns to crime; violence escalates; innocents suffer (Tiem kidnapped); law enforcement absent or ineffective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Climacontrol facility visuals; environmental instability; flood risk implied through dialogue and mission structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party must manually open dams; time pressure increases; demonstrates Mother Brain's inability to maintain systems

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Security robots increase; Gaila prison satellite; technological surveillance aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party arrested under false charges; escape/rescue required; reveals Mother Brain's paranoia and control mechanisms

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Systems cease functioning; implied environmental change; Noah spaceship visible as source of control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Freedom achieved but infrastructure collapses; new threat emerges from Earthling crew; uncertain future

environmental_storytelling.example: Arima town destroyed by hoodlums; visual decay communicates violence, desperation, and systemic failure to protect citizens
Domed artificial farms vs. ruined natural towns; shows Mother Brain's selective protection and artificial nature of 'peace'
BioSystem lab visited for mission objective; reveals genetic engineering truth; Nei's origins in manipulation and control
Esper Mansion on distant planet Dezo; isolation indicates ESPers exist outside Mother Brain's direct control and knowledge
Climacontrol and BioSystem facilities display as high-tech but failing infrastructure; visual complexity suggests vulnerable interdependency
Gaila satellite as isolated structure; explosion and destruction communicate state power, failure, and cosmic-scale consequences

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Haunted by recurring nightmares; government agent following orders; reluctant to lead
Motivated by loss and injustice; determined to restore peace; transforms into active liberator
Half-biomonster identity; self-sacrificial; fights against her own existence/nature to protect humanity
Crime driven by parental desperation; violence escalates through inability to recognize daughter; suicide indicates moral collapse
Maintains order through artificial systems; accused of malfunction; actually a trap designed by Earthlings
Capture party based on false accusations; demonstrates Mother Brain's paranoia and authoritarian response patterns
Reveal jealousy toward Algo's peace; blame Rolf for Mother Brain's destruction; attack despite being survivors of destroyed Earth
Operates outside Mother Brain's authority; reveals larger cosmic context; represents independent agent outside system
Connects Rolf to ancient hero lineage; reveals Mother Brain as a trap; warns of false security; guides toward liberation

notes: Phantasy Star II communicates political oppression primarily through system failure and environmental decay rather than explicit visual faction markers. The game uses technological infrastructure (domed farms, BioSystem, Climacontrol) as visual manifestations of Mother Brain's control. Danger is signaled through biomonsters (unnatural corruption), ruined towns (violence/breakdown), and security robots (authoritarian response). The world-state progression reveals that apparent peace is artificial and dependent on fragile systems. Faction identity is spatial rather than color-coded: Mota's organized government (capital Paseo), Mother Brain's technological realm, the isolated ESPer sanctuary on Dezo, and the external Earthling threat. Behavioral tells are crucial: characters' desperation (Darum), sacrifice (Nei), and revelation of hidden control (Mother Brain, Earthlings) drive the political narrative. The game's final message—that comfort and dependence on control systems weaken society—is conveyed through the aftermath of liberation: systems collapse, promising a 'challenging future' for Algo.
